blocks.content: Raesis returned to her office, draped in her red cloak, deep in thought.
Her aide, who had followed her in, cautiously asked, "Commander, what should we do about Shirone?"
If Shirone had shown his Nephilim abilities from the start, it wouldn't have been an issue. Nephilim were such beings.
But Shirone had already surpassed Raesis's potential.
This would inevitably affect the Nor rebels, who placed great faith in Spirit Force.
For Raesis, it was a tricky problem.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: I knew they didn't trust me, but I didn't expect them to stab me in the back like this.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Commander, your orders…?
The aide, growing impatient, pressed again.
Outside, the atmosphere was filled with curiosity about Shirone's rank.
The longer Raesis delayed, the more her absolute authority would be questioned.
Ha ha ha ha ha!
Raesis threw her head back and laughed, leaving the aide bewildered.
When she stopped laughing, she looked at the aide.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: I've finally found the perfect element to complement my flaws.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: So, Shirone…

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: Yes, I will absorb him. Shirone's immense Spirit Force will be a vessel for any biological trait. I can't miss this opportunity.
The aide's eyes darted around.
He had seen Shirone's Spirit Force with his own eyes, so he had no objections.
But Shirone was a symbol of the rebels.
If public opinion turned against them, everything they had built could collapse in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: I know what you're thinking.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: I'm sorry, but it's dangerous for you too, Commander.
Raesis swirled her cloak and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: We'll cultivate a clone from Shirone's genetic sample. After clinical trials on the clone, I'll absorb it once we're sure.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Is that possible? Shirone isn't a citizen. Human cloning is a sensitive field, and even slight differences in genetic sequences can lead to failure.
Citizens and non-citizens were both human, but they had evolved separately for a long time.
Even with the same number of chromosomes, differences in genetic sequences could make successful cloning unlikely.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: Even if it's possible, it could waste Black Elixir.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Don't worry. Humans are like machines. There are always ways to design them. I'm already excited about what abilities I can give Shirone.
Raesis's face lit up with excitement, then turned stern again.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: Make a deal with the military community. We need the genes of powerful monsters to combine with Shirone's clone. Only the strongest traits will be selected.

blocks.speaker: Aide
blocks.content: Understood.
After the aide left, Raesis sat on the sofa, crossing her legs and licking her lips.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: Experimenting on a Nephilim's body was unprecedented, but the potential rewards were limitless.

blocks.speaker: Raesis
blocks.content: This will be very interesting.
It was the gamble of Raesis's life.
Shirone was promoted to the leader of the Special Forces under the 2nd Headquarters.
His unit, the 3rd Mobile Unit, carried out direct orders from the commander, unlike the 1st and 2nd units.
The members were elite warriors with Spirit Forces over 20%.
Plu, now the deputy leader of the 3rd Mobile Unit, glanced at the three members.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Although her status had risen, she couldn't shake the feeling of being watched.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: They're annoying, aren't they?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: We expected this from the moment you opened the Immortal Function.
Raesis had promised an alliance with the 1st Headquarters if Shirone joined the 2nd Headquarters, but there were still concerns.
It was wise to prepare a foundation to shake the power balance in case of an emergency rather than fully trusting her.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Still…
When Plu turned, the three members watching Shirone quickly looked away.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: It"ll be hard to shake them off during missions. Maybe at night?
As Plu pondered, one of the members approached.
A tall man with a long philtrum, his eyes were intimidating despite his lower rank.

blocks.speaker: Member
blocks.content: Deputy Leader, we've received our first mission from the higher-ups. At the Plateau of Corruption…

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Wait. Why are you receiving the mission?
Plu interrupted.

blocks.speaker: Member
blocks.content: We haven't heard anything. The reporting system is a mess. Shirone is the leader.
The man remained expressionless, then reluctantly bowed his head.

blocks.speaker: Member
blocks.content: I'm sorry. There was a mistake. I was the leader before, so I received the orders. I'll report to the higher-ups to prevent this from happening again.
Plu couldn't push further since they had to work together for now.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Alright. Continue. At the Plateau of Corruption?
The drone translated Plu's words.
It wasn't fast enough for urgent wartime commands, but it worked for regular conversations.

blocks.speaker: Member
blocks.content: There's an ancient ruin at the Plateau of Corruption. The rebels call it the 'Tomb of Angels.' It was once frequented by angels but is now abandoned.
Heaven's history was ancient, even mythical to humanity.
But even in Heaven, there was an ancient past, which intrigued Shirone and Plu.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Hmm, what do we need to do there?

blocks.speaker: Member
blocks.content: The ancient ruins are sealed by angelic magic, so only angels or Nephilim can enter. Currently, Shirone is the only Nephilim among the rebels.
Raesis intended to monitor them while fully utilizing them.
Plu didn't know the relationship between the Nor and magicians, but Raesis's approach was certainly magician-like.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Alright, let's go.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I have to do this. The seniors are on more dangerous missions.
As the light spirit Elizer activated, the 3rd Mobile Unit was transported to the Plateau of Corruption.
Armin, Siina, Etella, and Kuan.
The four of them camped in the forest and entered the mainland early in the morning.
The mainland reminded them of a world's fair from their own world.
There was no uniformity, and strange creatures roamed the streets.
The buildings along the road varied widely, from huts and wooden houses to brick buildings and mechanical structures, reflecting different eras.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Wow, this is amazing. You won't see this anywhere else.
Etella smiled innocently, but there was no response.
With only four members left, the distance between Siina and Armin became more apparent.
Siina was the most troubled.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: She had expected Armin to explain why he had followed her to Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: If it were that easy, why hadn't he mentioned it before?

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Before she could finish her thoughts, Siina couldn't hold back her frustration.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Are you okay?
Etella, who had been trying to lighten the mood, fell silent, and Armin finally spoke.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Keira. What happened?
It was something he could never tell Siina.
But even beyond that, staying silent was a matter of respect for Keira.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: You're leaving the Ivory Tower?
She wasn't shocked, as she had expected it.
The problem was that Armin was bound by a pact with the Ivory Tower.
The pact, a power of the Regulators, would activate if the agreement was broken, preventing him from ever using magic again.
So Keira brought out the pact.
At the bottom of the document was Armin's signature, etched in his own hand.
He had signed it under duress, with Siina as leverage, hoping never to have to tear it up.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: If you leave, our relationship ends here. If you're okay with that, go.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Thank you for everything.
Armin knew Keira had supported him in many ways, even if she didn't show it.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Don't try to convince me. Don't act like you're the only one suffering! Or what? Are you going to stop time and run away again?
Armin shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: I can't use Stop Magic like that. I'm just a burden now.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: So what? If I tear up the pact, you're nothing. A blind man who can't even go to the bathroom alone!
Then that's how it is. I've caused enough trouble, so I'll let you have your way this time.
Armin walked towards the door.
The sound of paper tearing.
Armin turned in surprise.
The pact's conditions were met, but the target wasn't him.
Keira had to sign it to monitor Armin for the Ivory Tower, and now it was destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Go. It's settled now. If you care so much about her, go!

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Keira, I…

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Don't come near me!
Keira shouted.
She didn't want to be pitied in her final moments.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Go to Heaven and protect her. But…
Keira, her face streaked with tears, looked up.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Come back alive. Protect her, and come back alive, you bastard!
Armin turned away silently.
Keira was also a renowned magician of the Ivory Tower.
He didn't think her achievements were any less than his.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: I'll repay you.
Armin cast Flicker Magic and disappeared.
Armin's team arrived at the entrance of the military community, known as 'Yamaeng,' on the outskirts of the mainland.
The massive complex lived up to its name, with smoke rising from every chimney beyond the walls.
No need to ask further.
Armin walked to the gate.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Excuse me. Is this…
The guards at the gate closed ranks, blocking the path.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Stop. Who are you?
They wore tight suits and dark glasses, unlike anything seen in their world.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: We have a request to make here. Can we speak to the person in charge?
The gate opened, and about ten guards in similar suits blocked Armin's path with expressionless faces.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Go back. The boss is in a bad mood today.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: That's unfortunate. But we don't have much time either. At least pass on our message.
A burly guard approached, cracking his knuckles.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Do you even know where you are?
As the ten guards simultaneously exuded a menacing aura, an unsettling energy enveloped Armin's group.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: This…
All four of them realized it at the same time.
It was an aura they could never feel in their original world.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Be careful.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: These beings are not human.
